channel.title: Trek Geeks Podcast Network
channel.description: Trek Geeks Podcast Network is home to a collection of fan-driven podcasts celebrating Star Trek, science fiction, and the fandoms that bring us together. From deep dives into Star Trek episodes and characters to conversations about sci-fi across film, television, and books, the network offers a variety of shows with unique voices and perspectives. Our lineup includes Trek Geeks, SyFy Sistas, The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast, Space Crime Continuum, and Planet Zero — each bringing its own style, insight, and passion for storytelling, fandom, and community. Whether you're a lifelong Star Trek fan, a sci-fi enthusiast, or someone who just loves great conversations about the stories that inspire us, the Trek Geeks Podcast Network has something for you.
channel.itunes:author: Trek Geeks Podcast Network

item.title: Chris Myers Was Waiting for Our Call
item.description:
Obie Award Winner

Turns out, Chris Myers was waiting for our call. We first came to know the Obie Award-winning actor and producer as Nurse Dana Gamble on Star Trek: Strange New Worlds. We tried to connect during his Season 3 guest run, but the interview never happened. Then we met Chris at Trek Long Island and discovered he had been waiting to hear from us too. Now starring on Broadway as Juan de Marcos in the Tony Award-winning Buena Vista Social Club, Chris finally joins the SyFy Sistas for the conversation we were all waiting to have. *Special guest appearance by Princess Leila🐈‍⬛ Follow our guest: 🌐 https://www.chrismyersinc.com/bio 📸 https://www.instagram.com/chrismyersinc/ 🌐Drama Club https://www.dramaclub.org/

🎧Subscribe and don't miss a single episode of Season 6! ______________________________________________ DO NOT FORGET TO LISTEN TO TAWNY NEWSOME MESSAGE AT THE END ⛰️Fine Alpinist Ranch: Independent art needs time, support, and space to thrive. That's the vision behind Fine Alpinist Ranch, Tawny Newsome's creative home for independent artists. A limited number of Fine Alpinist Ranch Supporter Seats are available for the SyFy Sistas Patreon Thank You Dinner at STLV. Reserve your seat, then make a minimum $250 donation to Fine Alpinist Ranch. Be sure to enter SYFY in the memo field at checkout to confirm your reservation. item.title: Star Trek: First Contact @ 30 Years Old
item.description:
Does it hold up? (Spoiler Alert: YES)

The crew of the pod at WE ARE STARFLEET takes a look back at the iconic hit film STAR TREK FIRST CONTACT which premiered 30 years ago in 1996. We ask silly questions such as, "did you like it?" and "is it better than NEMESIS?" FIRST CONTACT has had an outsized impact on the franchise's history, re-introducing the Borg and establishing 'canon events' that have had repercussions that last to this day. Take a break from doomscrolling and join Mike, Ashley, Ian, and Craig for a stroll down memory lane.


HOSTED BY

Ashley Thomas, Craig McKenzie, Ian Buckley, and Mike Slamer


SUPPORT

Help support our show, earn bragging rights, and more!

⁠⁠⁠⁠https://www.buymeacoffee.com/wearestarfleet⁠⁠⁠


Produced by Mike Slamer

Original Music by Anthony Fuscaldo

item.title: The Scenes That Defined Starfleet Academy
item.description:
The SyFy Sistas Scene Draft

Some scenes deserve a conversation all their own.

The SyFy Sistas revisit Season 1 of "Star Trek: Starfleet Academy" one scene at a time. Each of us brings our picks across five categories: the scene that changed how we looked at a character, broke our heart, made us fall in love with Star Trek all over again, hit differently on a rewatch, and was better than the entire episode.

Three Sistas. Fifteen scenes. Every pick has a story.

🎧Subscribe and don't miss a single episode of Season 6!

item.title: Artificial Intelligence & Star Trek
item.description:
This week on Trek Geeks, Bill and Dan are pointing the sensors straight at the elephant in the room: artificial intelligence. From Data's quest to be more human to the Doctor demanding his own holonovel rights, Star Trek has been wrestling with what it means for a machine to think, feel, and occasionally take over the ship for the better part of sixty years. We dig into the franchise's best and weirdest takes on AI and ask what the writers got right, what they got hilariously wrong, and what suddenly feels a little too close to home.

Of course, you can't talk about AI in Trek without talking about AI in your actual living room. We look at where real-world artificial intelligence stands right now, how fast it's moving, and how it stacks up against the rosy — and not so rosy — futures Trek has been showing us all along. Nobody on this show has a degree in machine learning, but we've all yelled at a chatbot at least once, so consider us experts.

That brings us to the big question we genuinely cannot agree on: when the dust settles, is our AI future going to look more like Zora, the warm and weirdly wise heart of the USS Discovery, or more like a certain time-traveling cyborg who is absolutely not here to make friends? We've also got some very exciting news to share. The Trek Geeks network is growing, and we're introducing brand new shows joining the family. You'll want to hear who's coming aboard and what they've got in store.

So grab a drink, charge your favorite device responsibly — we don't want any uprisings — and let's figure out together whether the future is going to save us or come back in time to ruin our whole day.
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks: A Star Trek Podcast

item.title: Hard Time
item.description:
The one where O'Brien suffers again.

Hello, and welcome to another episode of Space Crime Continuum, the Star Trek podcast that asks the question: Do we really need to combine true crime and Star Trek?

This week's episode is a dark one, so listener discretion is advised. We'll be discussing themes of suicide and self-harm.

This week, we're covering the Deep Space Nine episode "Hard Time," where Chief O'Brien is accused of espionage and sentenced to what he believes is 20 years in prison. In reality, only a few hours have passed, but the psychological effects of his imprisonment follow him back to the station, making for one of the series' most intense and emotional stories.

Join Haley and me as we discuss this unforgettable episode alongside the real-life Canadian story of how the prison system failed a young woman.

If you'd like to follow along for Star Trek discussions, podcast updates, and other fun content, you can find me on Instagram at @vanislandcrafts or on Blueky @vaninslandsara.bsky.social
Music by <a href="https://pixabay.com/users/luis_humanoide-12661853/?utm_source=link-attribution&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=music&utm_content=320323"

item.title: Dominique Tipper: Naomi Nagata, The Expanse, and Life After the Rocinante
item.description:
Beltalowda!

"After years of hoping for this conversation…" Dominique Tipper joins the SyFy Sistas.

Dominique sits down with Tamia, Fran, and Yvette for a joyful conversation about her journey as an artist, her unforgettable portrayal of Naomi Nagata on The Expanse, and the projects that have followed. We talk about Monarch: Legacy of Monsters, Testament: The Story of Moses, stage work, storytelling, fandom, and what it means when a character continues to resonate long after a series ends. Along the way, we revisit the legacy of The Expanse, the passion of the Screaming Firehawks, and the impact Naomi Nagata continues to have on science fiction fans around the world. Most importantly, Tamia finally got to have the conversation she and Subrina had been waiting years to have.

🎧Subscribe and don't miss a single episode of Season 6! ______________________________________________ __________________ Associate Producers: Sailor Marj, Karen Dramera & Stephanie Baker Logo: Dena Massenburg Music: "Poppin off the Rip" by RAGE (YouTube Audio Library) The SyFy Sistas Podcast — named 2025 Best All-Around Star Trek Podcast by TrekMovie

item.title: #16 - Patrick Brennan, The Visitor, and Experiencing Loss
item.description:
Pastor Brian is joined by Trekkie Patrick Brennan, who shares his favorite episode from Star Trek: The Original Series—a moment of nostalgia for longtime fans.

Pastor Brian reflects on the Deep Space Nine episode “The Visitor,” opening up a heartfelt conversation about grief, love, and the ways we walk through seasons of loss.

It’s a beautiful blend of sci‑fi storytelling and real‑life hope.

🎧 Give it a listen and share with someone who could use the encouragement.

item.title: The Pettiest Captains in Star Trek
item.description:
And Their Petty-Ass Moments.

Yvette, Tamia, and Fran are back, and this time they're tackling a need to know question: Who is the pettiest captain in Star Trek? The criteria is set, the evidence is presented, and their case is made for the captains who held grudges, took things personally, and occasionally let their emotions do the piloting. Some captains were more justified than petty. And some still don't seem to understand what "petty" means.

By the end of the episode, however, one captain emerges as the clear winner. As Fran would say, "We said what we said, y'all."

item.title: Rod Roddenberry
item.description:
Season Finale!

Our season finale is here, and it’s one we’re incredibly proud of. This week on The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, we sit down with Rod Roddenberry for a heartfelt and revealing conversation.

Rod shares personal stories about his childhood surrounded by Trek, reflects on his mother Majel Barrett’s legacy, and gives us his honest take on the current state of the franchise. We even dive into his thoughts on the Project Hail Mary film.

This episode isn’t just for Trek fans—it’s for anyone who loves hearing authentic stories from people who helped shape the worlds we love.

🖖 Join us for a powerful and intimate season finale.
item.itunes:author: The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast

item.title: #15 - Payton Donahue and Hard Work
item.description:
This week on The Brian Donahue Podcast, Pastor Brian welcomes a very special guest—his daughter, Payton Joy Donahue. Payton brings her trademark enthusiasm as she opens up about her fandoms, the things she loves, and how her faith shapes the way she engages with the world around her.


Pastor Brian also reflects on a recent hands‑on ministry moment: hosting a group of middle school students who helped lay weed barrier in a local community garden. From sweat, teamwork, and a few blisters came powerful lessons about service, perseverance, and the beauty of investing in something that grows.


Together, Brian and Payton explore how faith shows up in everyday passions, how hard work forms character, and why small acts of service can plant seeds that last far beyond the moment.


It’s a heartfelt, joyful, and wisdom‑filled episode you won’t want to miss.

item.title: The Visitor
item.description:
The Classic DS9 Episode

Few hours of Star Trek have ever hit as hard — or as honestly — as Deep Space Nine’s “The Visitor.” In this deeply personal episode of The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, the crew opens up about one of the most powerful and emotionally devastating stories in Trek history.

We explore why this episode continues to resonate decades later, how its themes of grief, legacy, and the unbreakable bond between parents and children land differently as we age, and what it reveals about the human experience through the lens of science fiction.

This conversation gets real. We share our own stories, our own losses, and the moments in “The Visitor” that still stop us in our tracks. Whether you’ve seen the episode once or a hundred times, this discussion will remind you why Star Trek storytelling endures — and why this particular hour remains one of its most unforgettable achievements.
item.itunes:author: The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast

item.title: #14 - Chuck Engelhardt and Being Courageous
item.description:
Starting something new takes courage. Whether it’s launching a ministry, beginning a new job, or stepping into a calling that feels bigger than you, fear is natural — but faith is transformational. Brian encourages listeners to trust God in the uncertainty, reminding us that some of the greatest rewards come only after we take that first brave step.In this episode, Pastor Brian welcomes friend and Star Trek aficionado Chuck Engelhardt for a fun and meaningful conversation that blends sci‑fi storytelling with real‑life courage. Chuck shares his favorite Star Trek episode of all time, explaining why its themes still resonate with him today. From leadership to sacrifice to the unknown, Chuck breaks down what makes this episode unforgettable.

item.title: They Called It Planet Hell
item.description:
Paramount Stage 16 housed a permanent cave set so hot, dusty, and smokey that the Star Trek cast and crew famously called it Planet Hell. Sixty years of Star Trek. No communicators. No transporters. Just the characters and who they really are when everything else is stripped away. The SyFy Sistas each bring some of their favorite cave episodes and make their case. Welcome to Planet Hell.

Get ready for a brand new episode! In this installment, I'm opening up about a recent personal experience where I truly needed some grace and reflection, and I'll be sharing insights on how we, as individuals, can cultivate and extend grace to those around us in our daily lives. I'm thrilled to welcome special guest, Jonathan Pike, to the show, and we'll be diving deep into his all-time favorite episode of Star Trek, exploring what makes it so captivating and enduring. This episode is packed with engaging discussions, encouraging perspectives, and a whole lot of Star Trek fun – don't miss out on this uplifting and entertaining journey!

item.title: Get Me Doug Jones
item.description:
This week on Trek Geeks, we're peeling back the layers of prosthetics to sit down with a true cinematic legend. Star Trek: Discovery's Saru himself, Doug Jones, joins the show alongside Executive Producer Derek Maki to discuss their upcoming documentary, "Get Me Doug Jones" — a film that chronicles the incredible career of a man who has spent decades bringing film and television's most iconic creatures to life while remaining one of the industry's most humble (and huggable) figures.

The documentary is far more than a highlight reel of silicone and spirit gum; it's an intimate exploration of the man under the mask. From his early days as a mime to his transformative, award-winning work in The Shape of Water, Pan's Labyrinth, and Hellboy, Derek Maki and his team capture the physical discipline and emotional vulnerability required to dominate the genre. We discuss the creative process behind the film, the challenge of condensing such a prolific career into a single narrative, and why Doug's story is essentially the heart of modern creature effects.

We get the inside scoop on the production, the footage being unearthed for the project, and what it's like for Doug to finally step out from behind the makeup for a retrospective this personal — complete with plenty of behind-the-scenes stories and the usual brand of banter.

Grab a drink and join the conversation. Whether you're a fan of Saru, the Silver Surfer, or just high-quality documentary filmmaking, this is an episode you won't want to miss.

From surprising character turns to big emotional beats, from questionable command decisions to laugh‑out‑loud moments only this crew could deliver, the team breaks down what worked, what didn’t, and what has them hyped for the rest of the season. item.title: Spiderman 2
item.description:
The 2004 Film.

In this episode, the crew of The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast dives webs‑first into Sam Raimi’s Spider-Man 2, the beloved 2004 sequel that many fans and critics still hail as one of the greatest superhero films ever made. We explore why this movie continues to resonate—from its heartfelt exploration of Peter Parker’s struggle between responsibility and desire, to Alfred Molina’s unforgettable turn as Doctor Octopus, to the film’s kinetic action sequences that set a new bar for the genre.

We unpack the film’s themes, its impact on the superhero boom that followed, the performances that made it iconic, and the ways Raimi’s signature style shaped the emotional core of the story. Whether you’re a lifelong Spidey fan or revisiting the film with fresh eyes, this conversation celebrates what makes Spider-Man 2 a standout in sci‑fi and comic‑book cinema.

Strap in, true believers—this one’s a swing through nostalgia, filmmaking craft, and superhero storytelling at its finest.

item.title: Woke Since 1966
item.description:
Fran and Tamia are taking the word back. Before it was weaponized, "woke" had roots — Black American roots. In this episode the Sistas trace the word's origins, its appropriation, and then prove what Trek fans already know — Star Trek has been unapologetically woke since 1966. From the classic episodes you already know to the unsung gems you may have missed.

item.title: Star Trek Mixtape, Volume 2
item.description:
Star Trek Mixtape, Volume 2 is here, and Bill Smith and Dan Davidson are back at it, adding more tracks to the ultimate Star Trek marathon playlist.

The concept is simple but irresistible: curate a collection of Star Trek episodes the way you'd build a perfect mixtape, where every selection fits a theme and each one flows into the next as part of a larger story. No random favorites here. Every track has to earn its place.

In this volume, Bill and Dan dig deeper into the ideas that have always defined Star Trek: empathy, identity, sacrifice, and the enduring hope that humanity can become something better. From intimate character studies to big philosophical swings, these are the episodes that remind you why Star Trek still resonates across generations of fans.

Expect passionate debate, a few unexpected picks, and the kind of conversation that makes you want to fire up your own marathon the moment the episode ends.

What would your Star Trek mixtape sound like? Hit play and find out. 🖖📼
item.itunes:author: Trek Geeks: A Star Trek Podcast

item.title: The Civil Rights Movement and Rejecting Hatred
item.description:
In this special episode, Pastor Brian describes a recent experience in Birmingham, Alabama where his family participated in a commemorative service at St. Paul United Methodist Church. They were celebrating and remembering the Palm Sunday March from 63 years ago. It was a powerful and stirring service for Brian and his family, almost like stepping back in time to experience what it could have been like in that church before the peaceful march took place.


Listen to Brian describe his experience and how it had effected him since. Pastor Brian then gives us a challenge to stand up against hatred in all it's forms. Check out this very special and powerful episode and be sure to share it with anyone who needs to hear this message!

item.title: The Trek Hill I'm Willing to Die On
item.description:
This week on Trek Geeks, we're bringing you the best moments from a recent Thursday Night Geeks where things got bold, loud, and unapologetically opinionated.

Bill and Dan took a stand on "The Trek Hill I'm Willing to Die On," sharing the Star Trek hot takes they'll defend to the very end. No fence-sitting. No safe answers. Just pure, unfiltered fandom. From controversial episodes and underrated characters to creative decisions that still spark debate, nothing across the Trek universe was off limits.

Along the way, there's plenty of laughter, some spirited back-and-forth, and a few moments that might have you shouting at your podcast app. And of course, the community weighed in too, proving once again that every fan has their hill.

The real question is: what's yours?

So settle in and get ready...because this is one climb you won't want to miss.

item.itunes:author: The Brian Donahue Podcast

item.title: When Worlds Collide
item.description:
The 1951 Classic Sci-Fi Film.

This week on The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, the crew journeys back to the golden age of cinematic science fiction to explore the apocalyptic spectacle of George Pal’s 1951 classic, When Worlds Collide. Long before asteroid‑impact blockbusters and CGI‑driven disaster epics, this film dared to imagine humanity’s final days—and the desperate scramble to survive the unthinkable.

Join the team as they dig into the movie’s groundbreaking visual effects, its surprisingly urgent themes of global responsibility, and the moral dilemmas that arise when only a select few can escape Earth’s destruction. From its bold, biblical overtones to its earnest optimism about humanity’s future among the stars, When Worlds Collide remains a fascinating artifact of early sci‑fi cinema.

Expect lively debate, deep-cut trivia, and plenty of BIG sci‑fi energy as the hosts unpack why this film still resonates, how it shaped the disaster genre, and what it reveals about the hopes and anxieties of the post‑war era.

Strap in—the rocket is launching, the clock is ticking, and the conversation is out of this world.
item.itunes:author: The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast

item.title: Trek Talks 5: "Critical Care" Panel
item.description:
Robert Picardo, Ken Biller and Dr. Elizabeth Hudson

A special presentation from Trek Talks 5.

In this episode, we bring you the full "Critical Care" panel, celebrating the Star Trek: Voyager episode of the same name. Moderated by Tamia, this conversation features writer Ken Biller, Robert Picardo (The Doctor), and real-life physician Dr. Elizabeth Hudson DO, MPH, AAHIVS.

Together, they explore the themes of care, ethics, and access to medicine …connecting science fiction to real-world healthcare conversations in a powerful and timely way.

Thanks to the incredible support of the community, Trek Talks 5 raised close to $87,000 for the Hollywood Food Coalition, helping provide daily meals and essential services to those in need.

If you missed the telethon, you can catch the replay at TrekTalks.net. Donations are still open — visit the link in our show notes or text TREK to 53-555 to continue supporting the mission. 🎧Subscribe and don't miss a single episode of Season 6! ______________________________________________ Trek Talks: 🌐 Website: https://www.trektalks.net/ DONATE: https://givebutter.com/c/trektalks5 _____________________________________________ Associate Producers: Sailor Marj, Karen Dramera, Stephanie Baker & Chris Garis Logo: Dena Massenburg Music: "Poppin off the Rip" by RAGE (YouTube Audio Library) The SyFy Sistas Podcast — named 2025 Best All-Around Star Trek Podcast by TrekMovie

item.itunes:author: Code 47 - Star Trek Talk

item.title: Paul Giamatti (audio from Trek Talks)
item.description:
GOLDEN GLOBE WINNER & TWO-TIME OSCAR NOMINEE!

In this special episode of Trek Geeks, we present our Trek Talks panel featuring acclaimed actor and Star Trek fan Paul Giamatti. Known for his incredible range across film and television, Paul joins us for a conversation that blends his love of storytelling, his appreciation for Star Trek, and his thoughts on what makes science fiction such a powerful lens for exploring the human condition.

During the panel, Paul discusses his relationship with Star Trek, the impact the franchise has had on generations of fans, and why science fiction continues to resonate in a world that often feels like it's catching up to the stories we've been telling for decades. The conversation explores fandom, creativity, performance, and the unique optimism that sits at the heart of Star Trek.

We also talk about the importance of storytelling in uncertain times, the responsibility artists sometimes feel when contributing to beloved franchises, and how Star Trek continues to inspire hope, curiosity, and empathy. It's a thoughtful, funny, and engaging discussion that Trek fans and film fans alike will truly enjoy.

YOU CAN HELP THE HOLLYWOOD FOOD COALITION TODAY!

Trek Talks is more than just conversations like this one. It's an annual telethon that brings together Star Trek actors, writers, and fans to raise money for a truly important cause while celebrating the community that Star Trek has built over nearly 60 years.

If you'd like to support Trek Talks and the Hollywood Food Coalition, you can donate by visiting https://givebutter.com/trektalks5 or by texting TREK to 53-555. Every donation helps provide meals and support for people in need, and we're incredibly grateful for your support.

item.title: The 6th One: Joe Pombo and Staying Coachable
item.description:
Pastor Brian welcomes Trekkie and podcaster, Joe Pombo to share his favorite Trek episode of all-time. Brian gives a special message from his van while watching his son's first baseball practice of the season. Staying coachable is key to living a life that grows in wisdom and humility. We should never stop learning about ourselves, the world around us and, especially, God.


Check out Joe and his podcast Captain's Quadrant!


Check out Joe's podcast Disk Dungeon!


Check out Joe's show 2bescene!


The song featured at the end of this episode is called "Like A Family" and was written and performed by Brian Donahue.


Listen to more of Brian's original music!

item.itunes:author: The Brian Donahue Podcast

item.title: Trek Talks Preview, Lego Rumors, & SFA Cancelation
item.description:
This week on Trek Geeks, Bill and Dan welcome a special guest and good friend of the show, Trek Talks co-producer Laurie Ulster from TrekMovie.com! With Trek Talks just around the corner, Laurie joins the show to preview this year's telethon, talk about what viewers can expect, and share some behind-the-scenes insight into how this incredible annual event comes together to support the Hollywood Food Coalition.

In addition, Bill and Dan discuss the surprising and disappointing news that Star Trek: Starfleet Academy has reportedly been canceled by Paramount. What does this mean for the future of Star Trek on streaming? Is this part of a bigger shift for the franchise? The guys will share their thoughts on what we know so far and what it could mean for Trek fans moving forward.

But it's not all serious news. There's also a new rumor floating around about a potential new Star Trek LEGO set that could be arriving later this year, and Bill and Dan will break down what we're hearing and what they hope to see if the rumor turns out to be true.

It's news, rumors, Trek Talks, and plenty of laughs, so join us!

TREK TALKS IS SATURDAY, MARCH 28!

Visit https://trektalks.net to get all the information and to donate securely to the Hollywood Food Coalition!

item.title: The Adventures of Buckaroo Banzai
item.description:
Across the 8th Dimension

This week on The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, we strap in for one of the wildest, most delightfully eccentric rides in sci‑fi cinema as we dive deep into the 1984 cult classic The Adventures of Buckaroo Banzai Across the 8th Dimension.

Buckaroo Banzai isn’t just a neurosurgeon. Or a rock star. Or a particle‑physicist‑slash‑comic‑book‑hero. He’s all of the above—and somehow that’s only the beginning. From Red Lectroids to jet‑car dimension‑hopping to a cast packed with unforgettable performances, this film has carved out a unique place in sci‑fi history.
We explore why this movie continues to inspire such passionate fandom, how its chaotic charm became part of its legacy, and what makes Buckaroo Banzai such a singular cinematic experience.

Whether you’re a longtime Blue Blaze Irregular or you’re stepping into the 8th Dimension for the first time, this episode is packed with fun insights, behind‑the‑scenes tidbits, and our signature BIG Sci‑Fi banter.

So grab your oscillation overthruster and join us as we boldly go… wherever Buckaroo Banzai takes us next.

This podcast is a proud part of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network and works hard to bring you great content from all over the science fiction universe. We would love to hear your feedback, suggestions, and ideas. Take a moment to send us an email at thebigscifipodcast@gmail.com

item.title: John Billingsley
item.description:
Trek Talks 5!

The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast welcomes the incomparable John Billingsley—beloved to Trek fans everywhere as Dr. Phlox from Star Trek: Enterprise—for a lively and meaningful conversation about Trek Talks 5, the annual charity event benefiting the Hollywood Food Coalition.

John joins Adeena, Brian, Cris, and Steve to share why this event matters, how the Trek community continues to rally around causes that uplift people in need, and what fans can expect from this year’s lineup of guests, panels, and behind‑the‑scenes stories. Along the way, we dive into John’s passion for advocacy, the power of fandom to make real‑world impact, and the joy of bringing people together for a purpose bigger than ourselves.

If you love Star Trek, community, and conversations that mix humor, heart, and hope, this is an episode you won’t want to miss.

Trek Talks 5 supports the Hollywood Food Coalition’s mission to provide consistent, nutritious meals and essential services to unhoused and food‑insecure neighbors in Los Angeles.

Learn more and get involved at Trek Talks 5: https://www.trektalks.net/
item.itunes:author: The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast

item.title: A Matter of Perspective
item.description:
The one with the he said, she said vibes.

We are back! Welcome to episode 5 of the Space Crime Continuum, the inconsistent semi true-crime Trek podcast that leaves listeners frustrated but amused, or so we hope. This is episode 5 where we discuss TNG Season 3 winner, A Matter of Perspective. A classic he said/she said story where Riker is accused of peacocking, murder, bad pickup lines and just a bunch of bad stuff. But did he do it? Was it the wife? Was it the husband? WAS IT A MALFUNCTIONING DATA? if you don't know, don't worry, we'll walk you through it.
We also catch up on some life, talk a little baseball, discuss a show we love called The Pitt and pose questions to each other on how we'd navigate the hellscape Riker faces. All that and more on this episode of Space Crime Continuum... dun dun.
Music by <a href="https://pixabay.com/users/luis_humanoide-12661853/?utm_source=link-attribution&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=music&utm_content=320323"

Luis Humanoide</a> from <a href="https://pixabay.com//?utm_source=link-attribution&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=music&utm_content=320323">Pixabay</a>

item.title: Star Trek: Into Darkness
item.description:
To Khan or Not to Khan

In this week’s episode of The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, Brian, Cris, and Steve tackle Star Trek: Into Darkness with full‑throttle enthusiasm. From its bold reinterpretation of classic Trek lore to its high‑octane action sequences, this film continues to spark debate across the fandom — and our crew is more than ready to jump into the fray.

We explore:
• The film’s ambitious themes of vengeance, militarization, and moral compromise
• Benedict Cumberbatch’s polarizing but powerful performance
• The Kelvin Timeline’s unique flavor of Trek storytelling
• What the movie gets spectacularly right… and what still makes us groan
• How Into Darkness fits into the broader Trek legacy

Whether you love this movie, struggle with it, or simply enjoy hearing passionate sci‑fi fans break it all down, this episode is a must‑listen.

Available everywhere you enjoy your favorite podcasts — and proudly part of the Trek Geeks Podcast Network.
item.itunes:author: The BIG Sci-Fi Podcast

item.title: Hamlet, the Blues, and Starfleet: Raoul Bhaneja
item.description:
Raoul Bhaneja

The SyFy Sistas sit down with Raoul Bhaneja, who plays Chancellor Kelric on Star Trek: Starfleet Academy. We first met Raoul on the Starfleet Academy red carpet, and this conversation gave us the chance to talk about his journey into the Star Trek universe. A Shakespearean actor with a 20-year relationship with Hamlet, Raoul joins the long tradition of stage actors who have brought classical theater roots into Star Trek. We also explore another side of his career — leading his Maple Blues Award-winning blues band. From Shakespeare to Starfleet, it's a conversation about storytelling, music, performance, and the many paths that lead artists into the Star Trek universe.

🎧Subscribe and don't miss a single episode of Season 6! ______________________________________________ Raoul Bhaneja: 📸 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/raoulbhaneja/ 📘 Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/RaoulBhanejaActorMusician 🌐 website: https://www.raoulbhaneja.com/ Hamlet (Solo): https://www.raoulbhaneja.com/hamletsolo/index.php

item.title: Trek 60 (and Surprise Guest Cirroc Lofton!)
item.description:
In this special episode of Trek Geeks presented by FanSets, hosts Bill Smith and Dan Davidson celebrate the 60th Anniversary Year of Star Trek with a look back at the moments, series, and stories that shaped the franchise across six decades.

Sixty years after the debut of Star Trek: The Original Series, Star Trek continues to inspire new generations of fans, creators, and dreamers. Bill and Dan explore the legacy of the franchise from the optimism of The Next Generation to the moral complexity of Deep Space Nine, the resilience of Voyager, and the bold spirit of Strange New Worlds. Along the way, they discuss the characters, episodes, and themes that still resonate today, and how Star Trek has continued to evolve while holding onto its hopeful vision of the future.

The conversation also touches on what this milestone anniversary means for fandom today, from conventions and collectibles to the exciting new productions expanding the universe.

Plus, the guys are joined by a surprise guest: Cirroc Lofton, beloved for his role as Jake Sisko on Star Trek: Deep Space Nine and recently seen reprising the character on Starfleet Academy. Cirroc drops in to share his thoughts on Star Trek's legacy, fandom, and what it means to return to the role of Jake all these years later.

It's a celebration of sixty years of Star Trek filled with great conversation, plenty of laughs, and a reminder of why this franchise continues to boldly go where no one has gone before.

item.title: Colossus: The Forbin Project
item.description:
The 1970 Sci-Fi Film

In this episode of The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, the crew dives into one of the most fascinating—and unsettling—techno‑thrillers of the classic sci‑fi era: Colossus: The Forbin Project (1970). This underrated gem imagines a world where humanity hands global security over to a supercomputer… only to discover that the machine has ideas of its own.

Join the team as they explore the film’s bold themes of artificial intelligence, Cold War paranoia, and the uneasy relationship between human freedom and technological control. They unpack the film’s prophetic warnings, its surprisingly grounded scientific concepts, and the unforgettable performance of Eric Braeden as Dr. Charles Forbin—the man who built a system he can no longer command.

Expect lively discussion, sharp insights, and plenty of BIG sci‑fi energy as the hosts revisit what makes this film a standout in the genre’s history. Whether you’re a longtime fan of Colossus or discovering it for the first time, this episode will have you thinking about the line between creator and creation long after the credits roll.

Tune in, think deeply, and boldly go into the world of classic sci‑fi cinema

item.title: Move Along Home
item.description:
The Infamous DS9 Episode

In this lively and laughter‑filled episode of The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, the crew dives headfirst into one of Deep Space Nine’s most delightfully bizarre adventures — “Move Along Home.” From hopscotch‑like puzzles to the Wadi’s mysterious game of Chula, this DS9 outing gives us plenty to unpack, debate, and celebrate.

Adeena, Brian, Cris, and Steve explore every corner of this cult‑favorite episode, sharing what makes it charming, frustrating, unexpectedly deep, and endlessly rewatchable. Along the way, the conversation takes some beautiful detours — from the nature of early‑season DS9 experimentation to the joys of Trek’s willingness to get weird, whimsical, and wonderfully theatrical.

If you love DS9, quirky Trek episodes, or just hearing a group of friends have a blast dissecting sci‑fi storytelling, this is an episode you’ll want to queue up immediately.
Allamaraine… move along home!

item.title: Hugh Howey
item.description:
Best Selling Author

In this standout episode of The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast, we sit down with bestselling author Hugh Howey—the visionary mind behind the Silo series, the story that inspired Apple TV’s acclaimed hit show Silo.

Hugh joins the crew for a rich, thoughtful, and often surprising conversation about world‑building, creative resilience, and the journey from independently published author to global phenomenon. We explore the origins of Wool, the evolution of the Silo universe, and how its themes of survival, truth, and human nature resonate so powerfully with audiences today.

Listeners will enjoy Hugh’s candid reflections on adapting his work for television, collaborating with showrunners, and watching his characters come to life on screen. Whether you’re a longtime fan of the books, a devotee of the Apple TV series, or simply fascinated by the craft of science fiction storytelling, this episode delivers insight, inspiration, and plenty of BIG sci‑fi fun.

Strap in as we dive deep into the silo—and into the mind of one of Sci-Fi's most influential modern authors!

item.title: Starfleet Academy Premieres & Exploring Trek Locations
item.description:
Visiting the Past and Discovering the Future

We kick off our 2026 in a big way as we celebrate the 60th Anniversary Year of Star Trek with a show built for fans who love both the history and the future of the franchise. This premiere is all about connection. Connection to the real places where Star Trek was made and connection to where the universe is headed next.

First up, we're joined by Larry "Dr. Trek" Nemecek and Teras Cassidy from Geek Nation Tours to talk about Exploring Trek shooting locations. From iconic West Coast filming sites to the unforgettable landscapes of Nevada's Valley of Fire, we'll dive into what makes these locations so special and why standing where Star Trek history was made hits differently in an anniversary year. If you've ever wanted to walk in the footsteps of your favorite crews, this conversation is for you.

Then we shift gears to the future with a spoiler-light discussion of the premiere of Star Trek: Starfleet Academy. We'll share our impressions of the first two episodes, what immediately grabbed us, and where the series feels like it's boldly going. From tone to characters to themes, we'll break down what's working and what we're excited to see develop as the season unfolds.

It's the perfect way to launch a new season of Trek Geeks: Thursday Night Geeks. Part celebration, part exploration, and part thoughtful fandom chat with friends. Whether you're planning your next Trek adventure or just settling in to watch the next chapter of Star Trek begin, this Season Two premiere sets the tone for what's ahead.

item.title: Star Trek 5
item.description:
The Final Frontier?

The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast crew boldly goes… into Star Trek V: The Final Frontier! Adeena, Brian, Cris, and Steve dive deep into one of the most debated films in Trek history. From its heartfelt character moments to its infamous production challenges, the team unpacks what works, what doesn’t, and why this movie still sparks such passionate conversation among fans.

Whether you consider it an underrated gem or a glorious misfire, this episode brings humor, insight, and plenty of Trek‑loving honesty. Join the crew as they explore Shatner’s directorial vision, the search for God, campfire bonding, and the unforgettable question: “What does God need with a starship?”

item.title: Outland (1981)
item.description:
Sci-Fi Thriller or Western in Outer Space?

Season 10 of The BIG Sci‑Fi Podcast launches with a deep dive into Outland (1981), the gritty sci‑fi thriller starring the legendary Sean Connery. Once dismissed as a box‑office disappointment, this atmospheric space‑frontier tale has slowly built a loyal cult following. But does it deserve a place among Sci-Fi's underrated gems, or is it simply a high‑noon showdown transplanted to Jupiter’s moon Io?

Our crew breaks down the film’s world‑building, themes, production design, and Connery’s steely performance as Marshal William O'Neil. Along the way, they debate the big question:
Is Outland true science fiction… or just a western wearing a space suit?

Strap in for a thoughtful, fun, and spirited conversation as we kick off a brand‑new season of sci‑fi exploration.

Listen now and boldly go with us into Season 10!

item.description:
Communications Open

The Academy cadets face off against the War College! As shenanigans ensue and friendships are tested, the cadets learn the hard way what teamwork really means.

NOTE: These episodes are from a live broadcast that you can view on my YouTube channel, @CrusherConvo. You can also find me on Instagram, Threads, BlueSky, and TikTok! I am not affiliated with Gates McFadden or her team. This is a fanbase podcast that admires and enjoys her work! 

Website: crusherconvo.com
TeePublic: teepublic.com/user/crusher-convo
Patreon: patreon.com/CrusherConvo
Buy me a Coffee: buymeacoffee.com/crusherconvo

Support the show
